commands.review_summary:
The PR adds bounded nearest-candidate diagnostics for edit-tool oldText mismatch errors in `src/agents/sessions/tools/edit.ts` and adds regression tests in `src/agents/sessions/tools/edit.test.ts`.

PR surface: Source +241, Tests +109. Total +350 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a strict bug reproduction because this is an ergonomics feature PR; source inspection confirms current main lacks candidate-line diagnostics. The review blockers are reproducible from the PR head's `check-lint` log and the missing real-behavior proof in the PR body.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the branch needs contributor proof and maintainer selection among competing implementations; automation cannot provide the contributor's real setup proof.

target.title: improve: show edit mismatch candidate lines
target.body:
﻿Closes #97032

## What Problem This Solves

Resolves a problem where edit tool `oldText` mismatch failures only showed the generic exact-match error plus current file contents, leaving users to guess whether indentation, escaping, or nearby text drift caused the miss.

## Why This Change Was Made

The edit mismatch path now appends bounded nearest-candidate diagnostics with line numbers, escaped line diffs, and indentation hints when the failing edit can be identified. Matching behavior is unchanged: the tool still requires the same exact/fuzzy edit semantics, and validation-phase no-op mismatch errors avoid candidate guesses when the indexed edit source is ambiguous.

## User Impact

Users and agents get actionable feedback after a failed edit call: the error can point at nearby candidate lines and show the visible difference, while still preserving the existing current-file-content hint.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/tools/edit.test.ts` - passed, 24 tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts` - passed, 65 tests
- `git diff --check` - passed
- `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings reported
